summary: Restrict approval audit scope configuration to admins and repair stale reviewed-timesheet revision node states.

notes: Approval audit scope writes are now restricted to platform admin role checks at the RLS layer.
Employee/system-management writers can no longer insert, update, or delete approval audit scopes.
Final approved, locked, or summarized timesheets with stale rejected/revision runtime nodes are repaired by cancelling only those stale node states while preserving assignee action history.
V0.18.30's manifest omitted migration 129 even though the tag includes it; this manifest records the complete migration set in the current release line.
Non-admin employee editors no longer see approval audit scope controls.
Employee saves from non-admin users no longer send auditScopeOrgIds.
The frontend API rejects audit scope payloads from non-admin users as a defense-in-depth guard.
Approval-center reviewed-period refreshes keep previous data visible while showing refresh/loading feedback.

verification: Run npm --prefix frontend run build with VITE_APP_VERSION=V0.18.31 and production VITE variables.
Run deploy/scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh on the cloud host with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.31.
Run scripts/smoke-frontend-render.mjs against production with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.31.
Run scripts/smoke-timesheet-withdraw.sql on the cloud host after deployment.
Run production RLS probes confirming manager audit-scope writes are rejected and admin writes are allowed in a rollback transaction.
known_warnings: Vite may still warn that some chunks are larger than 500 kB.
Migration 130 is a one-time data repair and should be reviewed through its metadata marker if audit history needs inspection.
